does: This paper introduces TeleAI-Safety, a comprehensive framework and benchmark for evaluating large language model safety against jailbreak attacks. The work integrates 19 attack methods, 29 defense mechanisms, and 19 evaluation approaches into a modular, reproducible system. It provides a curated corpus of 342 adversarial samples spanning 12 risk categories and conducts systematic evaluations across 14 target models. The framework addresses the fragmentation in existing safety research by combining previously isolated components—attack generation, defense application, and effectiveness measurement—into a unified platform that enables standardized, reproducible safety assessments across different LLM architectures.
connects: This work builds on decades of adversarial robustness research in machine learning, extending concerns about adversarial examples and prompt injection to large language models. It responds to recent proliferation of jailbreaking techniques and defenses that have emerged in parallel studies without systematic comparison, including works on prompt-based attacks, instruction-following vulnerabilities, and various defense strategies like fine-tuning and filtering. The paper addresses recognized gaps in the safety evaluation literature, particularly the lack of unified benchmarks comparable to standardized adversarial robustness evaluation frameworks used in computer vision and NLP. It synthesizes insights from prior work on LLM safety, red-teaming methodologies, and robust evaluation practices.
enables: This comprehensive framework enables future research to conduct fair, reproducible cross-study comparisons of attack and defense effectiveness against jailbreaking. It provides a foundation for developing new attack and defense methods with standardized evaluation protocols, reducing the overhead of independent safety assessment. The modular design allows researchers to isolate and study individual components—attacks, defenses, and evaluation metrics—in controlled settings. The work supports the development of improved evaluation methods specific to LLM safety and enables systematic risk categorization across diverse failure modes. By establishing this benchmark infrastructure, it facilitates broader adoption of rigorous safety testing in industry deployments and supports regulatory compliance efforts requiring demonstrated robustness against known attack classes.
